Implement the following plan: # Add Skeleton Loading State to Draft List ## Context The DraftList page flickers on visit because the loading state is just plain text ("Loading drafts..."). Replacing it with skeleton placeholder cards will eliminate the visual jump. ## Changes ### 1. `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/DraftList/DraftList.jsx` (lines 67-74) Replace the plain text loading state with 3 skeleton draft item placeholders: ```jsx if (isLoading) { return (

Drafts

{[1, 2, 3].map((i) => (
))}
); } ``` ### 2. `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/DraftList/DraftList.css` Add skeleton styles at end of file, matching the shimmer pattern used in `RelatedPosts.css`: - `.draft-skeleton-item` — same padding/layout as `.draft-list-item` - `.draft-skeleton-title` — 60% width, 12px height bar with shimmer - `.draft-skeleton-date` — 30% width, 10px height bar with shimmer (delayed) - `@keyframes draft-shimmer` — opacity pulse 0.4 to 0.8 (same as `rp-shimmer`) ## Verification - Visit the drafts page — should see 3 shimmer rows instead of "Loading drafts..." text - Once loaded, skeleton replaced by actual draft items or empty state (no flicker) If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: C:\Users\johnm\.claude\projects\C--Users-johnm-iskrib-v3\b00ce88b-da7b-493d-b58d-4322d392d87c.jsonl

# Add Skeleton Loading State to Draft List ## Context The DraftList page flickers on visit because the loading state is just plain text ("Loading drafts..."). Replacing it with skeleton placeholder cards will eliminate the visual jump. ## Changes ### 1. `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/DraftList/DraftList.jsx` (lines 67-74) Replace the plain text loading state with 3 skeleton draft item placeholders: ```jsx if (isLoading) { return (

Drafts

{[1, 2, 3].map((i) => (
))}
); } ``` ### 2. `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/DraftList/DraftList.css` Add skeleton styles at end of file, matching the shimmer pattern used in `RelatedPosts.css`: - `.draft-skeleton-item` — same padding/layout as `.draft-list-item` - `.draft-skeleton-title` — 60% width, 12px height bar with shimmer - `.draft-skeleton-date` — 30% width, 10px height bar with shimmer (delayed) - `@keyframes draft-shimmer` — opacity pulse 0.4 to 0.8 (same as `rp-shimmer`) ## Verification - Visit the drafts page — should see 3 shimmer rows instead of "Loading drafts..." text - Once loaded, skeleton replaced by actual draft items or empty state (no flicker)

content.text: Done. The draft list now shows 3 shimmer skeleton rows while loading instead of plain "Loading drafts..." text. The skeleton items match the layout of real draft items (title bar at 60% width, date bar at 30% width) with a staggered opacity pulse animation.
